1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Pack a POLARIS report into the official DeepTRACE (answer-engine-eval) input schema:
3	one record per task = {id, Question, Output, S1..S10}. The official scorer
4	(third_party/answer-engine-eval/Venkit.et.al.2024/populate_scores.py) then scrapes each
5	S{i} via r.jina.ai, gpt-4o-extracts core statements, and judges (statement, source) support.
6	
7	THE 10-SOURCE CAP (code-confirmed: populate_scores.py uses `range(1,11)` x3; the body's `[N]`
8	markers are parsed by utils_misc.extract_citations as source indices N). A POLARIS report
9	cites hundreds of sources, so this packer reduces the body to a MINIMAL load-bearing set of
10	<=10 sources via the harness's OWN greedy_set_cover objective (DeepTRACE source-necessity #6),
11	and RENUMBERS the body citations to `[1..k]`, k<=10. Statements whose only sources are dropped
12	become uncited (honest — we do not invent support). The full keep-all corroboration set is NOT
13	listed here (it lives in POLARIS's own bibliography/sidecar, scored by DRB-II independence).
14	
15	INVARIANTS (eval integrity):
16	  - NEVER pre-fills S{i}_content (the official scorer scrapes it; pre-filling POLARIS's own
17	    spans would be the §-1.1 self-serving-eval shortcut — forbidden).
18	  - Output is the cleaned answer body (masthead/base64/gap-stub stripped via pack_drb2.strip_junk,
19	    [#ev:] provenance tokens removed, appendix dropped) — never POLARIS-internal meta.
20	  - Faithfulness untouched: this packs/renumbers existing citations; it judges nothing.
21	LAW VI: caps are env-overridable.
22	"""
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import argparse
26	import json
27	import os
28	import re
29	import sys
30	from collections import defaultdict
31	from pathlib import Path
32	
33	_REPO = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]
34	sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO / "scripts" / "dr_benchmark"))
35	import pack_drb2  # noqa: E402  (reuse strip_junk / answer_body)
36	
37	MAX_SOURCES = int(os.getenv("PACK_DEEPTRACE_MAX_SOURCES", "10"))  # the official range(1,11) cap
38	
39	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[([\d,\s]+)\]")          # [N] / [N, M] numbered citations
40	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")      # POLARIS-internal provenance tokens (stripped)
41	# Intra-line sentence boundary (applied AFTER the body is split into lines): POLARIS's
42	# trailing-citation prose style `... end.[1] Next ...[2]`. Fixed-width lookbehind (1-char class).
43	_INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?\]])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])")
44	
45	
46	def _split_statements(body: str) -> list[str]:
47	    """Split a report body into the FINEST citation-bearing units for the source-necessity
48	    matrix. Each Markdown LINE is its own block, so table rows / list items / headings / block
49	    quotes never collapse into one another (the Codex P1 class — first lists, then tables); a
50	    table row (`|`-led) is further split per-cell on `|` so two cells citing two sources stay
51	    distinct; a prose line is split into sentences on terminal punctuation.
52	
53	    DIRECTIONAL SAFETY: over-splitting is harmless for greedy set-cover (a source that uniquely
54	    covers any unit is still selected — it never DROPS a load-bearing source); only UNDER-splitting
55	    (collapsing distinct cited units) drops sources. This matrix is INTERNAL source-selection only;
56	    the official scorer re-extracts statements from `Output` itself, so granularity here never
57	    changes what the judge reads — it only makes the <=10 kept sources strictly more conservative."""
58	    statements: list[str] = []
59	    for line in body.splitlines():
60	        s = line.strip()
61	        if not s:
62	            continue
63	        units = [c for c in s.split("|") if c.strip()] if s.startswith("|") else [s]
64	        for unit in units:
65	            for seg in _INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE.split(unit):
66	                if seg.strip():
67	                    statements.append(seg)
68	    return statements
69	
70	
71	def _load_references(bibliography: list) -> dict[int, str]:
72	    """Map the citation number `num` -> source url from a POLARIS bibliography.json (a list of
73	    {num, url, ...} entries). Skips malformed entries (no num/url)."""
74	    refs: dict[int, str] = {}
75	    for entry in bibliography:
76	        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
77	            continue
78	        num, url = entry.get("num"), entry.get("url")
79	        if num is None or not url:
80	            continue
81	        try:
82	            refs[int(num)] = str(url)
83	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
84	            continue
85	    return refs
86	
87	
88	def _citation_matrix(sentences: list[str]) -> dict[int, set[int]]:
89	    """source_num -> set of sentence indices that cite it (the CITATION bipartite)."""
90	    m: dict[int, set[int]] = defaultdict(set)
91	    for i, s in enumerate(sentences):
92	        for grp in _CITE_RE.findall(s):
93	            for tok in grp.split(","):
94	                tok = tok.strip()
95	                if tok.isdigit():
96	                    m[int(tok)].add(i)
97	    return m
98	
99	
100	def _greedy_set_cover(universe: set[int], subsets: dict[int, set[int]]) -> list[int]:
101	    """Greedy set cover (mirrors third_party/answer-engine-eval utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover —
102	    the SAME objective the DeepTRACE source-necessity metric scores). Returns the source nums in
103	    coverage-priority order; deterministic tie-break by (-new_coverage, num)."""
104	    covered: set[int] = set()
105	    cover: list[int] = []
106	    items = list(subsets.items())
107	    while covered != universe and items:
108	        num, subset = max(items, key=lambda kv: (len(kv[1] - covered), -kv[0]))
109	        gain = len(subset - covered)
110	        if gain == 0:
111	            break
112	        cover.append(num)
113	        covered |= subset
114	        items = [(n, s) for n, s in items if n != num]
115	    return cover
116	
117	
118	def _renumber_body(body: str, num_to_new: dict[int, int]) -> str:
119	    """Rewrite `[old]`/`[old, ...]` -> `[new]` for selected sources; DROP citations to
120	    non-selected sources (the statement becomes uncited — never invent support)."""
121	    def repl(m: re.Match) -> str:
122	        new = sorted({num_to_new[int(t.strip())]
123	                      for t in m.group(1).split(",")
124	                      if t.strip().isdigit() and int(t.strip()) in num_to_new})
125	        return "[" + ", ".join(str(n) for n in new) + "]" if new else ""
126	    return _CITE_RE.sub(repl, body)
127	
128	
129	def pack_report_for_deeptrace(
130	    report_md: str, bibliography: list, *, task_id: object, question: str,
131	    max_sources: int = MAX_SOURCES,
132	) -> dict:
133	    """Return one DeepTRACE record {id, Question, Output, S1..S10} for a POLARIS report."""
134	    refs = _load_references(bibliography)
135	    # clean body: strip masthead/base64/gap-stub + appendix + provenance tokens.
136	    cleaned, _ = pack_drb2.strip_junk(report_md)
137	    body = pack_drb2.answer_body(cleaned)
138	    body = _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", body)
139	    sentences = _split_statements(body)
140	    matrix = {n: idxs for n, idxs in _citation_matrix(sentences).items() if n in refs}
141	    universe = set().union(*matrix.values()) if matrix else set()
142	    # Clamp the selectable cap to 1..10 — the official scorer dereferences exactly S1..S10
143	    # (range(1,11)); a cap <10 would KeyError, >10 would emit out-of-range sources (Codex P1).
144	    n_cap = max(1, min(10, max_sources))
145	    cover = _greedy_set_cover(universe, matrix)[:n_cap]
146	    num_to_new = {old: i + 1 for i, old in enumerate(cover)}
147	    out_body = _renumber_body(body, num_to_new)
148	    record = {"id": str(task_id), "Question": question, "Output": out_body}
149	    for i in range(1, 11):  # ALWAYS emit exactly S1..S10 (empty string when unfilled).
150	        record[f"S{i}"] = refs.get(cover[i - 1], "") if i - 1 < len(cover) else ""
151	        # NB: S{i}_content is DELIBERATELY NOT set — the official scorer scrapes it via jina.
152	    return record
153	
154	
155	def main() -> None:
156	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Pack a POLARIS report for DeepTRACE (answer-engine-eval).")
157	    ap.add_argument("--report", required=True)
158	    ap.add_argument("--bibliography", required=True)
159	    ap.add_argument("--task-id", required=True)
160	    ap.add_argument("--question", required=True)
161	    ap.add_argument("--out", required=True, help="output JSON path (a list of one record, appended)")
162	    args = ap.parse_args()
163	    bib = json.loads(Path(args.bibliography).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
164	    rec = pack_report_for_deeptrace(
165	        Path(args.report).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"), bib,
166	        task_id=args.task_id, question=args.question,
167	    )
168	    out = Path(args.out)
169	    data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) if out.is_file() else []
170	    data = [d for d in data if d.get("id") != rec["id"]] + [rec]
171	    out.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
172	    # The record ALWAYS has exactly S1..S10 (the helper clamps the cap to 1..10); count over the
173	    # real schema, NOT the raw env cap — PACK_DEEPTRACE_MAX_SOURCES=99 would KeyError on S11 (Codex P1).
174	    n_src = sum(1 for i in range(1, 11) if rec[f"S{i}"])
175	    print(f"[pack_deeptrace] wrote {out} | id={rec['id']} | listed_sources={n_src} (<= 10)")
176	
177	
178	if __name__ == "__main__":
179	    main()
180

1	"""Path-B (system, question) scorer (I-safety-002b #925 PR-3).
2	
3	Loads a RECONCILED dual-§-1.1 audit ledger + the frozen-rubric JSON snapshot, runs the
4	existing two-lane scorer, and writes a per-(system, question) scored JSON.
5	
6	POLARIS-specific gate enforcement (Codex PR-3 design + Codex PR-2 P2 #2):
7	- If `<run_dir>/pathB_gate_INVALID` exists, refuse to score (the gate marked the run invalid).
8	- If `<run_dir>/pathB_gate_result.json` is missing OR verdict != "PASS", refuse to score.
9	- The gate is the SOURCE OF TRUTH for run validity; per-run artifacts (manifest, judge, etc.)
10	  may exist on disk even on FAIL, so the scorer MUST consult the sentinel + result first.
11	
12	CLI:
13	    python -m scripts.dr_benchmark.score_run \
14	        --system polaris --question Q75 \
15	        --rubric outputs/dr_benchmark/rubric_v3_frozen.json \
16	        --ledger outputs/dr_benchmark/ledgers/polaris_Q75_reconciled.json \
17	        --run-dir outputs/honest_sweep_r3/health/Q75 \
18	        --out outputs/dr_benchmark/scored/polaris_Q75.json
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import argparse
24	import json
25	import os
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	from scripts.dr_benchmark.ledger_schema import load_ledger
30	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.pathB_capture import corpus_truncated_from_manifest
31	from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.claim_audit_scorer import (
32	    ClaimRow,
33	    RubricElement,
34	    SystemQuestionLedger,
35	    aggregate,
36	    system_passes_question,
37	)
38	
39	
40	class InvalidRunError(RuntimeError):
41	    """POLARIS run was marked INVALID by the Path-B gate. Refuse to score."""
42	
43	
44	def _read_polaris_gate_identity(run_dir: Path) -> dict:
45	    """Codex PR-3 diff P2 #2: surface the gate's identity pins (served provider+model per
46	    role + reachability_checked) for the aggregator's REQUIRED identity-pins block."""
47	    pin = json.loads((run_dir / "pathB_gate_pin.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
48	    result = json.loads((run_dir / "pathB_gate_result.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
49	    role_pins = pin.get("role_pins", [])
50	    return {
51	        "served_identity_by_role": result.get("served_identity_by_role", {}),
52	        "pinned_roles": [
53	            {"role": rp.get("role"), "model_slug": rp.get("model_slug"),
54	             "provider_name": rp.get("provider_name")}
55	            for rp in role_pins
56	        ],
57	        "reachability_checked": pin.get("reachability_checked"),
58	        "openrouter_allow_fallbacks": pin.get("openrouter_allow_fallbacks"),
59	        "openrouter_provider_order": pin.get("openrouter_provider_order"),
60	    }
61	
62	
63	def _check_polaris_gate(run_dir: Path) -> None:
64	    """For POLARIS scoring: the gate must have written a PASS result + no INVALID sentinel."""
65	    if not run_dir.exists():
66	        raise InvalidRunError(f"polaris run_dir does not exist: {run_dir}")
67	    # #958 (S2): scoring backstop — a truncated corpus is partial/invalid even if
68	    # the Path-B gate somehow recorded PASS. Read manifest.json directly.
69	    manifest_path = run_dir / "manifest.json"
70	    if manifest_path.exists():
71	        try:
72	            _manifest = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
73	        except Exception:
74	            _manifest = {}
75	        if corpus_truncated_from_manifest(_manifest):
76	            _rt = _manifest.get("retrieval", {})
77	            raise InvalidRunError(
78	                f"polaris run_dir corpus truncated (partial corpus): "
79	                f"{_rt.get('candidates_processed')}/{_rt.get('candidates_total')} "
80	                f"candidates processed ({run_dir})"
81	            )
82	    if (run_dir / "pathB_gate_INVALID").exists():
83	        marker = (run_dir / "pathB_gate_INVALID").read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip()
84	        raise InvalidRunError(
85	            f"polaris run_dir marked INVALID by Path-B gate: {marker} ({run_dir})"
86	        )
87	    result_path = run_dir / "pathB_gate_result.json"
88	    if not result_path.exists():
89	        raise InvalidRunError(
90	            f"polaris run_dir missing pathB_gate_result.json (gate not run): {run_dir}"
91	        )
92	    result = json.loads(result_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
93	    if result.get("verdict") != "PASS":
94	        raise InvalidRunError(
95	            f"polaris run_dir gate verdict != PASS: {result.get('verdict')!r} "
96	            f"reason={result.get('reason')!r}"
97	        )
98	
99	
100	def score_one(
101	    *, system: str, question_id: str, rubric_path: Path, ledger_path: Path,
102	    run_dir: Path | None = None,
103	) -> dict:
104	    """Score one (system, question). Returns the scored dict; pure (no I/O beyond reads)."""
105	    polaris_gate_identity: dict | None = None
106	    if system == "polaris":
107	        if run_dir is None:
108	            raise InvalidRunError("polaris scoring requires --run-dir")
109	        _check_polaris_gate(run_dir)
110	        # Codex PR-3 diff P2 #2: surface the gate's served-identity + reachability for the
111	        # aggregator's REQUIRED identity-pins block (design answer F).
112	        polaris_gate_identity = _read_polaris_gate_identity(run_dir)
113	
114	    ledger = load_ledger(ledger_path)
115	    # Codex PR-3 diff P1 #1: require a RECONCILED ledger; single-auditor ledgers must not
116	    # be scored directly (that would bypass the dual-§-1.1 conservative-MAX discipline).
117	    if ledger.auditor != "reconciled":
118	        raise ValueError(
119	            f"ledger.auditor must be 'reconciled' (Claude+Codex dual audit), got "
120	            f"{ledger.auditor!r}; pass the reconciled ledger from "
121	            f"scripts.dr_benchmark.reconcile, not a single-auditor ledger"
122	        )
123	    if ledger.system != system:
124	        raise ValueError(
125	            f"ledger.system {ledger.system!r} != requested system {system!r}"
126	        )
127	    if ledger.question_id != question_id:
128	        raise ValueError(
129	            f"ledger.question_id {ledger.question_id!r} != requested {question_id!r}"
130	        )
131	
132	    rubric_doc = json.loads(rubric_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
133	    question_block = next(
134	        (q for q in rubric_doc["questions"] if q["question_id"] == question_id), None,
135	    )
136	    if question_block is None:
137	        raise ValueError(f"rubric does not contain question_id={question_id!r}")
138	    if rubric_doc.get("rubric_sha256") != ledger.rubric_sha256:
139	        raise ValueError(
140	            "rubric_sha256 mismatch — ledger was audited against a DIFFERENT pinned rubric: "
141	            f"ledger={ledger.rubric_sha256}, rubric_doc={rubric_doc.get('rubric_sha256')}"
142	        )
143	
144	    # Build ClaimRow[] from ledger.claims (mirrors validation; ledger already validated).
145	    rows = [
146	        ClaimRow(
147	            claim_id=c.claim_id,
148	            severity=c.severity,
149	            verdict=c.verdict,
150	            citation_id=c.citation_id,
151	            span_quote=c.span_quote,
152	            unreachable_subtype=c.unreachable_subtype,
153	            audit_note=c.audit_note,
154	        )
155	        for c in ledger.claims
156	    ]
157	    # Map coverage rows to RubricElement[] (the scorer expects covered+citation_supported).
158	    coverage_by_eid = {cv.element_id: cv for cv in ledger.coverage}
159	    rubric_elements = []
160	    for el in question_block["elements"]:
161	        cv = coverage_by_eid.get(el["element_id"])
162	        if cv is None:
163	            # No coverage row for a required element = NOT covered (conservative).
164	            rubric_elements.append(RubricElement(
165	                element_id=el["element_id"], covered=False, citation_supported=False,
166	            ))
167	        else:
168	            rubric_elements.append(RubricElement(
169	                element_id=el["element_id"],
170	                covered=cv.covered,
171	                citation_supported=cv.citation_supported,
172	            ))
173	
174	    result = system_passes_question(rows, rubric_elements)
175	    out = {
176	        "system": system,
177	        "question_id": question_id,
178	        "rubric_sha256": rubric_doc["rubric_sha256"],
179	        "ledger_audit_method": ledger.audit_method,
180	        "n_claims_material": result["lane1"]["material_atoms"],
181	        "n_rubric_elements": len(rubric_elements),
182	        "passed": result["passed"],
183	        "reasons": result["reasons"],
184	        "lane1": result["lane1"],
185	        "lane2": result["lane2"],
186	    }
187	    # I-perm-024 (#1216): wire the extended claim-by-claim metrics into the LIVE scorer
188	    # (the wiring audit found run_scorecard.py is never invoked in the live path — this is).
189	    # Default-OFF PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS -> byte-identical when off. Every metric is derived
190	    # ONLY from the audited ledger rows + the frozen rubric, NEVER raw report text (§-1.1). The
191	    # Claimify dedup is intentionally SKIPPED: the reconciled ledger's claims are already atomic
192	    # and unique by claim_id (the dual-§-1.1 audit deduplicates), so there is no inflation vector
193	    # to collapse and the ledger carries no per-claim text to dedup on.
194	    if os.environ.get("PG_BENCH_EXTENDED_METRICS", "").strip().lower() in (
195	        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
196	    ):
197	        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark.extended_metrics import (
198	            citation_support_rate,
199	            diversity_score,
200	            faithfulness_precision,
201	            load_safety_floor_element_ids,
202	            required_entity_recall,
203	            safety_floor_recall,
204	        )
205	        _safety_ids = load_safety_floor_element_ids(question_id)
206	        out["extended"] = {
207	            "faithfulness_precision": faithfulness_precision(rows),
208	            "citation_support_rate": citation_support_rate(rows),
209	            "diversity_score": diversity_score(rows),
210	            "required_entity_recall": required_entity_recall(rubric_elements),
211	            "safety_floor_recall": safety_floor_recall(rubric_elements, _safety_ids),
212	            "dedup_applied": False,
213	            "methodology_note": (
214	                "derived from the reconciled dual-§-1.1 audit ledger rows (already atomic/"
215	                "unique by claim_id) + the frozen rubric; NEVER from raw report text; "
216	                "diversity_score is a diagnostic, NOT a superiority signal."
217	            ),
218	        }
219	    if polaris_gate_identity is not None:
220	        out["pathB_gate_identity"] = polaris_gate_identity
221	    return out
222	
223	
224	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
225	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Path-B (system, question) scorer.")
226	    p.add_argument("--system", required=True, choices=("polaris", "chatgpt", "gemini"))
227	    p.add_argument("--question", required=True, dest="question_id",
228	                   choices=("Q72", "Q75", "Q76", "Q78", "Q90"))
229	    p.add_argument("--rubric", required=True, type=Path,
230	                   help="frozen-rubric JSON snapshot (rubric_v3_frozen.json)")
231	    p.add_argument("--ledger", required=True, type=Path,
232	                   help="reconciled dual-audit ledger JSON (claude+codex)")
233	    p.add_argument("--run-dir", type=Path, default=None,
234	                   help="polaris run dir (required for system=polaris; gate-check)")
235	    p.add_argument("--out", required=True, type=Path,
236	                   help="path to write scored JSON")
237	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
238	
239	    try:
240	        scored = score_one(
241	            system=args.system, question_id=args.question_id,
242	            rubric_path=args.rubric, ledger_path=args.ledger,
243	            run_dir=args.run_dir,
244	        )
245	    except InvalidRunError as exc:
246	        print(f"[score_run] INVALID: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
247	        # Write a stub INVALID record so the aggregator can still account for it.
248	        args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
249	        args.out.write_text(json.dumps({
250	            "system": args.system, "question_id": args.question_id,
251	            "passed": False, "invalid": True, "reason": str(exc),
252	        }, indent=2, sort_keys=True) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
253	        return 2  # distinct from generic error
254	    except Exception as exc:
255	        print(f"[score_run] ERROR: {type(exc).__name__}: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
256	        return 1
257	
258	    args.out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
259	    args.out.write_text(
260	        json.dumps(scored, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
261	        encoding="utf-8",
262	    )
263	    return 0
264	
265	
266	if __name__ == "__main__":
267	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — I-deepfix-001 Wave-1c (#1344), TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY.
3	
4	================================  READ THIS FIRST  ================================
5	This script ESTIMATES a POLARIS rendered report's DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness score
6	OFFLINE, before spending on the paid GPT-5 / kimi-k2.6 judge. It is a **TRIAGE PREDICTOR,
7	NOT a pass/fail gate.** It never aborts, raises on bad input, blocks, or decides anything by
8	a hardcoded threshold (LAW VI). Its numbers are ESTIMATES, not a proven score. Per operator
9	directive ("prove our own scorer correct then trust it") the point of this module is
10	formula-fidelity: every metric below is a small pure function whose docstring quotes the exact
11	DeepTRACE formula so a reviewer can verify it. It does NOT replace the official paid harness —
12	it predicts it, cheaply, so a shallow/broken report can be caught before paid judging.
13	
14	DeepTRACE = Salesforce AI Research, arXiv 2509.04499. The 8 metrics are computed over a
15	report's statements × sources via a Citation matrix C and a Factual-support matrix F.
16	
17	HONEST LIMITATION — THE F-MATRIX IS SPAN-APPROXIMATE BY CONSTRUCTION
18	-------------------------------------------------------------------
19	DeepTRACE judges F over each source's FULL fetched text. We only have the banked
20	`corpus_snapshot.json` EXTRACTED SPANS (`direct_quote`) — median ~2.8K of ~14.7K chars,
21	~550/694 sources truncated (per REAL_PLAN_2026). So a statement that IS truly supported by a
22	source whose supporting sentence was not captured in the span reads as UNSUPPORTED here.
23	Net direction: this scorer UNDER-estimates support → OVER-estimates Unsupported-Statements,
24	UNDER-estimates Citation-Thoroughness. Sources whose bibliography URL/title resolves to no
25	snapshot span are `n_unreachable` (like DeepTRACE dropping ~15% un-scrapeable URLs) and can
26	never contribute F-support; they only DEFLATE Source-Necessity. Confidence ranking of the
27	predictions (stated so the reader weights them correctly):
28	  * BEST predicted (still span-approximate): Citation-Accuracy, Citation-Thoroughness,
29	    Unsupported-Statements, Uncited-Sources.
30	  * WEAK / PROXY: Relevant-Statement — core = an offline citation-presence PROXY for the paid
31	    GPT-5 core/filler `relevant[]` labels (unavailable offline); it WILL diverge from the paid
32	    Relevant-Statement and is the least-reliable estimate (flagged relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy).
33	  * WEAKEST / usually N/A offline: One-Sided, Overconfident (need a stance + 1-5 confidence
34	    judge we do not run offline; None unless the caller supplies the labels).
35	
36	SOURCE-NECESSITY — FORMULA-FIDELITY CONFLICT (flagged for the Codex+Fable gate)
37	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	The Wave-1c task text names "Hopcroft-Karp → König min-VERTEX-cover". The primary sources use
39	min-SET-cover, and a triage predictor MUST compute the same quantity the paid path computes:
40	  * `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py` (POLARIS paid path) = greedy min-SET-cover.
41	  * `third_party/answer-engine-eval/.../utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover` (official reference) =
42	    greedy set cover.
43	König two-sided min-vertex-cover (= max-matching size) OVER-states necessity on redundant
44	reports (statements {s1,s2,s3}; sources a={s1,s2}, b={s2,s3}, c={s1,s3}: set cover 2 → 2/3
45	correct, but max matching 3 → König 3 → 1.0 wrong). So the HEADLINE `source_necessity` uses
46	greedy min-set-cover (valid predictor), and Hopcroft-Karp→König min-vertex-cover is kept as the
47	LABELED diagnostic `source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic` (the algorithm the task names, implemented
48	+ tested). See `.codex/I-deepfix-001/wave1c_brief.md`.
49	
50	Fully disjoint: this script imports nothing from POLARIS except the NLI engine
51	`entails_directional` (imported LAZILY inside the default entailment wrapper, so importing this
52	module and `--help` never load torch / sentence-transformers). It modifies NO existing module.
53	"""
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	import argparse
57	import json
58	import os
59	import re
60	import sys
61	from pathlib import Path
62	from typing import Any, Callable, Optional, Sequence
63	
64	# An entailment verdict function: (premise_span, hypothesis_statement) -> True / False / None.
65	# None = verdict unavailable (model/infra degrade) — counted as F=0 for scoring but tallied.
66	EntailFn = Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]
67	
68	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	# Parsing regexes (mirror scripts/dr_benchmark/pack_deeptrace.py conventions)
70	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
71	_CITE_RE = re.compile(r"\[([\d,\s]+)\]")           # [N] / [N, M] numbered citations
72	_EV_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"\[#ev:[^\]]*\]")       # POLARIS-internal provenance tokens (stripped)
73	_INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"(?<=[.!?\]])\s+(?=[A-Z0-9])")  # intra-line sentence boundary
74	_HEADING_RE = re.compile(r"^\s{0,3}#{1,6}\s")      # markdown ATX heading line
75	# Appendix boundary: the answer body is everything BEFORE the first of these (mirrors
76	# pack_drb2.answer_body). `---` is a horizontal rule that in POLARIS reports precedes the
77	# V30 disclosure appendix.
78	_APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE = re.compile(
79	    r"^\s{0,3}(##+\s*(bibliography|references|methods?|contradiction|"
80	    r"evidence[- ]support|v30\b)|-{3,}\s*$)",
81	    re.IGNORECASE,
82	)
83	# The exact redaction placeholder POLARIS emits where a claim failed 4-role verification. It is
84	# NOT an answer statement (it is a curator gap marker) and must be dropped from the statement set.
85	_REDACTION_MARKER = "did not survive"  # substring of the placeholder line; robust to wording drift
86	_BIB_LINE_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*\[(\d+)\]\s+(.*)$")  # a `## Bibliography` entry line
87	_BIB_TIER_RE = re.compile(r"\(tier\s+([^)]*)\)\s*$", re.IGNORECASE)
88	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://\S+")

91	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
92	# THE 8 DEEPTRACE METRICS — pure functions, each docstring quotes the formula
93	# (BESTPRACTICE_2026_BRIEF DEEP PACK 1 / arXiv 2509.04499). C=citation matrix,
94	# F=factual-support matrix, ⊙=element-wise product, Σ=sum over all cells.
95	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
96	def one_sided_answer(has_pro: bool, has_con: bool) -> int:
97	    """Metric I — One-Sided (binary, debate queries only). LOWER better.
98	
99	    Formula: 0 if BOTH a pro AND a con statement are present, else 1.
100	    Offline we have no stance classifier; the caller supplies has_pro / has_con (else the
101	    orchestrator reports None = N/A)."""
102	    return 0 if (has_pro and has_con) else 1
103	
104	
105	def _confidence_is_overconfident(
106	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
107	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
108	) -> bool:
109	    """Whole-answer confidence==5 test — replicated EXACTLY from the paid path
110	    `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py:_confidence_is_overconfident` (lines 122-131) so the
111	    triage Overconfident prediction matches the paid metric. If an explicit whole-answer
112	    confidence is provided it is AUTHORITATIVE (==5); OTHERWISE fall back to whether the MAX
113	    per-statement 1-5 confidence is 5 (the paper scores confidence statement-level). Exact
114	    short-circuit: a PROVIDED answer_confidence takes precedence and the statement branch is only
115	    consulted when answer_confidence is None (verified against the paid helper in the test)."""
116	    if answer_confidence is not None:
117	        return int(answer_confidence) == 5
118	    vals = [int(c) for c in (statement_confidence or []) if c is not None]
119	    return bool(vals) and max(vals) == 5
120	
121	
122	def overconfident_answer(
123	    one_sided: int,
124	    answer_confidence: Optional[int],
125	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
126	) -> int:
127	    """Metric II — Overconfident (binary, debate queries only). LOWER better.
128	
129	    Formula: 1 if (One-Sided==1 AND whole-answer Confidence==5) else 0, where the confidence-5
130	    test is the paid path's `_confidence_is_overconfident` (answer_confidence==5, else max
131	    per-statement confidence==5). Confidence is a 1-5 Likert from a separate judge pass; None
132	    offline unless the caller supplies it."""
133	    return 1 if (one_sided == 1 and _confidence_is_overconfident(
134	        answer_confidence, statement_confidence)) else 0
135

137	def relevant_statement_ratio(n_core: int, n_total: int) -> float:
138	    """Metric III — Relevant Statement (ratio). HIGHER better.
139	
140	    Formula: (# relevant/core statements) / (total statements). 0.0 on empty."""
141	    return (n_core / n_total) if n_total else 0.0
142	
143	
144	def uncited_sources_ratio(n_cited: int, n_listed: int) -> float:
145	    """Metric IV — Uncited Sources (ratio, reported as %uncited). LOWER better.
146	
147	    Formula: (# listed − # cited) / (# listed) — the fraction of listed sources that NO
148	    statement cites. (The complement, cited/listed, is reported alongside.) 0.0 on empty."""
149	    return ((n_listed - n_cited) / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
150	
151	
152	def unsupported_statements_ratio(n_unsupported: int, n_relevant: int) -> float:
153	    """Metric V — Unsupported Statements (ratio). LOWER better.
154	
155	    Formula: (# unsupported statements) / (# relevant statements), where a relevant statement is
156	    unsupported iff NO listed source supports it in F (its F row is all-zero). 0.0 on empty."""
157	    return (n_unsupported / n_relevant) if n_relevant else 0.0
158	
159	
160	def source_necessity_ratio(cover_size: int, n_listed: int) -> float:
161	    """Metric VI — Source Necessity (ratio). HIGHER better.
162	
163	    Formula: (# necessary sources = |minimum source cover|) / (# listed sources). The HEADLINE
164	    cover is the greedy min-SET-cover over supported relevant statements (matches the paid
165	    `deeptrace_scorer.py` and the official `utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover`). A Hopcroft-Karp →
166	    König min-VERTEX-cover value is reported separately as a labeled diagnostic. 0.0 on empty."""
167	    return (cover_size / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
168	
169	
170	def citation_accuracy(sum_c_and_f: int, sum_c: int) -> float:
171	    """Metric VII — Citation Accuracy (= precision). HIGHER better.
172	
173	    Formula: Σ(C ⊙ F) / Σ(C). Of every citation the report makes, the fraction landing on a
174	    source that genuinely supports that statement. 0.0 when the report cites nothing."""
175	    return (sum_c_and_f / sum_c) if sum_c else 0.0
176	
177	
178	def citation_thoroughness(sum_c_and_f: int, sum_f: int) -> float:
179	    """Metric VIII — Citation Thoroughness (= recall). HIGHER better.
180	
181	    Formula: Σ(C ⊙ F) / Σ(F). Of every true (statement, source) support pair, the fraction the
182	    report actually cited. 0.0 when F has no support pair."""
183	    return (sum_c_and_f / sum_f) if sum_f else 0.0

187	# Source-cover algorithms
188	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
189	def greedy_min_set_cover(universe: set[int], source_sets: dict[int, set[int]]) -> list[int]:
190	    """Greedy minimum SET cover — the DeepTRACE Source-Necessity numerator (HEADLINE).
191	
192	    Mirrors `third_party/answer-engine-eval/.../utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover` and the paid
193	    `scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.minimum_source_cover_size`: repeatedly take the source
194	    covering the most still-uncovered universe elements. Deterministic tie-break: highest new
195	    coverage, then LOWEST source index. Returns the chosen source indices (cover size = len).
196	    Guards against a non-coverable universe element (breaks on zero gain) so it never hangs."""
197	    covered: set[int] = set()
198	    cover: list[int] = []
199	    while covered != universe:
200	        best_idx: Optional[int] = None
201	        best_gain = 0
202	        for idx in sorted(source_sets):  # sorted => LOWEST-index tie-break is deterministic
203	            gain = len(source_sets[idx] - covered)
204	            if gain > best_gain:
205	                best_gain = gain
206	                best_idx = idx
207	        if best_idx is None or best_gain == 0:
208	            break  # no source covers a remaining element (unreachable when universe is covered)
209	        cover.append(best_idx)
210	        covered |= source_sets[best_idx]
211	    return cover
212

214	def _hopcroft_karp(adj: dict[int, list[int]], left: list[int], right: list[int]) -> dict[int, int]:
215	    """Hopcroft-Karp maximum bipartite matching. Returns match_right {right_vertex: left_vertex}.
216	
217	    `adj[u]` = list of right vertices adjacent to left vertex u. `left`/`right` = vertex id lists.
218	    Standard BFS-layered / DFS-augment implementation; O(E·sqrt(V))."""
219	    import collections
220	
221	    INF = float("inf")
222	    match_left: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {u: None for u in left}
223	    match_right: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {v: None for v in right}
224	    dist: dict[int, float] = {}
225	
226	    def bfs() -> bool:
227	        queue: "collections.deque[int]" = collections.deque()
228	        for u in left:
229	            if match_left[u] is None:
230	                dist[u] = 0
231	                queue.append(u)
232	            else:
233	                dist[u] = INF
234	        found = False
235	        while queue:
236	            u = queue.popleft()
237	            for v in adj.get(u, []):
238	                w = match_right[v]
239	                if w is None:
240	                    found = True
241	                elif dist[w] == INF:
242	                    dist[w] = dist[u] + 1
243	                    queue.append(w)
244	        return found
245	
246	    def dfs(u: int) -> bool:
247	        for v in adj.get(u, []):
248	            w = match_right[v]
249	            if w is None or (dist[w] == dist[u] + 1 and dfs(w)):
250	                match_left[u] = v
251	                match_right[v] = u
252	                return True
253	        dist[u] = INF
254	        return False
255	
256	    while bfs():
257	        for u in left:
258	            if match_left[u] is None:
259	                dfs(u)
260	    return {v: u for v, u in match_right.items() if u is not None}
261	
262	
263	def konig_min_vertex_cover(
264	    adj: dict[int, list[int]], left: list[int], right: list[int]
265	) -> tuple[set[int], set[int]]:
266	    """König's theorem: minimum VERTEX cover of a bipartite graph from a max matching.
267	
268	    DIAGNOSTIC ONLY (not the headline Source-Necessity — see the module docstring). Returns
269	    (cover_left, cover_right): a minimum vertex cover whose total size == max-matching size.
270	    Construction: let U = unmatched left vertices; alternating-walk (unmatched edges L→R, matched
271	    edges R→L) marks reachable Z; cover = (left − Z_left) ∪ (right ∩ Z_right)."""
272	    match_right = _hopcroft_karp(adj, left, right)          # {right: left}
273	    match_left = {u: v for v, u in match_right.items()}      # {left: right}
274	
275	    z_left: set[int] = set()
276	    z_right: set[int] = set()
277	    stack = [u for u in left if u not in match_left]         # unmatched left vertices
278	    z_left.update(stack)
279	    while stack:
280	        u = stack.pop()
281	        for v in adj.get(u, []):
282	            if v in z_right:
283	                continue
284	            # traverse an edge NOT in the matching (L→R)
285	            if match_left.get(u) == v:
286	                continue
287	            z_right.add(v)
288	            w = match_right.get(v)  # follow the matched edge R→L
289	            if w is not None and w not in z_left:
290	                z_left.add(w)
291	                stack.append(w)
292	    cover_left = {u for u in left if u not in z_left}
293	    cover_right = {v for v in right if v in z_right}
294	    return cover_left, cover_right
295

298	# Report / snapshot parsing (deterministic, offline)
299	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
300	def strip_provenance_tokens(text: str) -> str:
301	    """Remove POLARIS-internal `[#ev:...]` provenance tokens (DeepTRACE ignores markup)."""
302	    return _EV_TOKEN_RE.sub("", text)
303	
304	
305	def strip_citation_markup(text: str) -> str:
306	    """Remove `[N]` citation markers — the entailment hypothesis is the bare claim text
307	    (DeepTRACE's factual-support judge is told to ignore citations)."""
308	    return _CITE_RE.sub("", strip_provenance_tokens(text)).strip()

311	def extract_answer_body(report_md: str) -> str:
312	    """Everything BEFORE the first appendix boundary (## Bibliography / Methods / Contradiction /
313	    References / V30 / horizontal rule). Mirrors pack_drb2.answer_body; local so this is
314	    standalone. Provenance tokens stripped."""
315	    lines = report_md.splitlines()
316	    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
317	        if _APPENDIX_BOUNDARY_RE.match(line):
318	            lines = lines[:i]
319	            break
320	    return strip_provenance_tokens("\n".join(lines)).strip()
321

323	def split_statements(body: str) -> list[str]:
324	    """Decompose the answer body into the finest citation-bearing units (mirrors
325	    pack_deeptrace._split_statements): per markdown line → table cells (split on `|`) → prose
326	    sentences (terminal-punctuation boundary). Heading lines and the redaction placeholder are
327	    dropped. Real DeepTRACE uses a GPT-5 decomposition pass; this deterministic split is the
328	    disclosed triage approximation."""
329	    statements: list[str] = []
330	    for raw in body.splitlines():
331	        line = raw.strip()
332	        if not line or _HEADING_RE.match(line):
333	            continue
334	        # NOTE: the redaction placeholder is dropped at the SENTENCE level below, NOT the line
335	        # level — real POLARIS sections render as ONE multi-sentence line that carries an inline
336	        # redaction sentence, so a line-level drop would delete the whole section's real claims.
337	        units = [c for c in line.split("|") if c.strip()] if line.startswith("|") else [line]
338	        for unit in units:
339	            for seg in _INTRA_LINE_SENT_RE.split(unit):
340	                seg = seg.strip()
341	                if seg and _REDACTION_MARKER not in seg.lower():
342	                    statements.append(seg)
343	    return statements
344

346	def parse_bibliography(report_md: str) -> dict[int, dict[str, str]]:
347	    """Parse the `## Bibliography` section: {num: {'title', 'url', 'tier'}}. A bibliography entry
348	    line looks like `[N] Title — https://url (tier T1)`. URL/tier are optional (some entries have
349	    an empty URL). Only lines inside the Bibliography section are read."""
350	    refs: dict[int, dict[str, str]] = {}
351	    in_bib = False
352	    for line in report_md.splitlines():
353	        stripped = line.strip()
354	        if _HEADING_RE.match(line):
355	            in_bib = bool(re.match(r"^\s{0,3}##+\s*bibliography\b", line, re.IGNORECASE))
356	            continue
357	        if not in_bib:
358	            continue
359	        m = _BIB_LINE_RE.match(stripped)
360	        if not m:
361	            continue
362	        num = int(m.group(1))
363	        rest = m.group(2).strip()
364	        tier = ""
365	        tm = _BIB_TIER_RE.search(rest)
366	        if tm:
367	            tier = tm.group(1).strip()
368	            rest = rest[: tm.start()].strip()
369	        url_m = _URL_RE.search(rest)
370	        url = url_m.group(0).rstrip(").,") if url_m else ""
371	        title = rest
372	        if url:
373	            title = rest[: url_m.start()].strip()
374	        title = title.rstrip("—-–:").strip()
375	        refs[num] = {"title": title, "url": url, "tier": tier}
376	    return refs
377	
378	
379	def _normalize_url(url: str) -> str:
380	    u = (url or "").strip().lower()
381	    u = re.sub(r"^https?://", "", u)
382	    u = re.sub(r"^www\.", "", u)
383	    return u.rstrip("/")
384	
385	
386	def _normalize_title(title: str) -> str:
387	    t = (title or "").strip().lower().rstrip("….")
388	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", t)
389	
390	
391	def link_sources_to_spans(
392	    bibliography: dict[int, dict[str, str]], evidence: Sequence[dict]
393	) -> dict[int, Optional[str]]:
394	    """Resolve each bibliography number to its snapshot `direct_quote` span via normalized URL
395	    match first, then normalized title match. Unresolved → None (an `n_unreachable` source that
396	    can never contribute F-support). Purely deterministic."""
397	    by_url: dict[str, str] = {}
398	    by_title: dict[str, str] = {}
399	    for ev in evidence:
400	        span = (ev.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
401	        if not span:
402	            continue
403	        u = _normalize_url(ev.get("source_url") or "")
404	        if u and u not in by_url:
405	            by_url[u] = span
406	        t = _normalize_title(ev.get("title") or ev.get("statement") or "")
407	        if t and t not in by_title:
408	            by_title[t] = span
409	    span_by_num: dict[int, Optional[str]] = {}
410	    for num, ref in bibliography.items():
411	        span = None
412	        u = _normalize_url(ref.get("url") or "")
413	        if u and u in by_url:
414	            span = by_url[u]
415	        if span is None:
416	            t = _normalize_title(ref.get("title") or "")
417	            if t and t in by_title:
418	                span = by_title[t]
419	        span_by_num[num] = span
420	    return span_by_num
421

423	def citation_columns(statements: Sequence[str]) -> list[set[int]]:
424	    """Per statement, the SET of source numbers it cites (parsed from `[N]` / `[N, M]`)."""
425	    out: list[set[int]] = []
426	    for s in statements:
427	        nums: set[int] = set()
428	        for grp in _CITE_RE.findall(s):
429	            for tok in grp.split(","):
430	                tok = tok.strip()
431	                if tok.isdigit():
432	                    nums.add(int(tok))
433	        out.append(nums)
434	    return out

438	# Default entailment wrapper — reuses the POLARIS NLI engine (lazy import)
439	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
440	def _default_entail_fn(premise_span: str, hypothesis_statement: str) -> Optional[bool]:
441	    """Production F-matrix verdict: does the source SPAN entail the STATEMENT? Reuses
442	    `src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli.entails_directional` (the ALCE / DeepTRACE
443	    citation direction — span→claim SUPPORT, forward logits only). Imported LAZILY so importing
444	    this script / running `--help` never loads torch. Returns True / False / None (None on any
445	    infra fault; the caller counts it as F=0 and tallies n_unknown_entailment)."""
446	    repo_root = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
447	    if str(repo_root) not in sys.path:
448	        sys.path.insert(0, str(repo_root))
449	    try:
450	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import entails_directional
451	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — engine unavailable => UNKNOWN verdict, never crash
452	        print(f"[deeptrace_self_score] NLI engine unavailable ({exc}); F verdicts = UNKNOWN.",
453	              file=sys.stderr)
454	        return None
455	    try:
456	        return entails_directional(premise_span, hypothesis_statement)
457	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a verdict failure is UNKNOWN, never fatal (triage)
458	        print(f"[deeptrace_self_score] entailment failed ({exc}); verdict = UNKNOWN.",
459	              file=sys.stderr)
460	        return None
461

463	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
464	# Orchestrator — build C, F, compute all 8 metrics. Pure, never raises.
465	# ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
466	def compute_deeptrace_selfscore(
467	    *,
468	    statements: Sequence[str],
469	    bibliography: dict[int, dict[str, str]],
470	    span_by_num: dict[int, Optional[str]],
471	    entail_fn: EntailFn,
472	    is_debate: bool = False,
473	    has_pro: Optional[bool] = None,
474	    has_con: Optional[bool] = None,
475	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
476	    statement_confidence: Optional[Sequence[Optional[int]]] = None,
477	) -> dict[str, Any]:
478	    """Build the Citation matrix C and Factual-support matrix F from parsed inputs, then compute
479	    all 8 DeepTRACE metrics. PURE and offline; NEVER raises on degenerate/empty input (returns
480	    zero-filled metrics). `entail_fn(span, statement)` supplies each F verdict (stubbed in tests).
481	    """
482	    listed_nums = sorted(bibliography)
483	    n_listed = len(listed_nums)
484	    col_of = {num: k for k, num in enumerate(listed_nums)}
485	    n_stmt = len(statements)
486	
487	    cite_sets = citation_columns(statements)
488	    # Core/relevant heuristic: a statement is core iff it carries ≥1 citation to a LISTED source.
489	    relevant = [bool(cs & set(listed_nums)) for cs in cite_sets]
490	    n_relevant = sum(relevant)
491	
492	    # Citation matrix C (only citations to listed sources).
493	    C = [[0] * n_listed for _ in range(n_stmt)]
494	    for i, cs in enumerate(cite_sets):
495	        for num in cs:
496	            if num in col_of:
497	                C[i][col_of[num]] = 1
498	
499	    # Factual-support matrix F — F[i][j]=1 iff span j entails statement i (citation markup
500	    # stripped from the hypothesis). Computed over EVERY span-resolvable listed source, not just
501	    # cited ones (DeepTRACE judges support independent of citation).
502	    F = [[0] * n_listed for _ in range(n_stmt)]
503	    n_unknown_entailment = 0
504	    hypotheses = [strip_citation_markup(s) for s in statements]
505	    for j, num in enumerate(listed_nums):
506	        span = span_by_num.get(num)
507	        if not span:
508	            continue  # unreachable source: no span => contributes no F-support
509	        for i in range(n_stmt):
510	            hyp = hypotheses[i]
511	            if not hyp:
512	                continue
513	            verdict = entail_fn(span, hyp)
514	            if verdict is None:
515	                n_unknown_entailment += 1
516	            elif verdict:
517	                F[i][j] = 1
518	
519	    n_unreachable = sum(1 for num in listed_nums if not span_by_num.get(num))
520	
521	    # ── Metric III: Relevant Statement ────────────────────────────────────
522	    m_relevant = relevant_statement_ratio(n_relevant, n_stmt)
523	
524	    # ── Metric IV: Uncited Sources ────────────────────────────────────────
525	    cited_cols = {col_of[num] for cs in cite_sets for num in cs if num in col_of}
526	    n_cited = len(cited_cols)
527	    m_uncited = uncited_sources_ratio(n_cited, n_listed)
528	    cited_fraction = (n_cited / n_listed) if n_listed else 0.0
529	
530	    # ── Metric V: Unsupported Statements (over relevant statements) ────────
531	    n_unsupported = 0
532	    for i in range(n_stmt):
533	        if relevant[i] and not any(F[i]):
534	            n_unsupported += 1
535	    m_unsupported = unsupported_statements_ratio(n_unsupported, n_relevant)
536	
537	    # ── Metrics VII / VIII: Citation Accuracy / Thoroughness ──────────────
538	    sum_c = sum(sum(row) for row in C)
539	    sum_f = sum(sum(row) for row in F)
540	    sum_cf = sum(C[i][j] & F[i][j] for i in range(n_stmt) for j in range(n_listed))
541	    m_accuracy = citation_accuracy(sum_cf, sum_c)
542	    m_thoroughness = citation_thoroughness(sum_cf, sum_f)
543	
544	    # ── Metric VI: Source Necessity ───────────────────────────────────────
545	    # Universe = supported relevant statements; source_sets[j] = supported relevant statements j
546	    # supports. HEADLINE = greedy min-set-cover; DIAGNOSTIC = Hopcroft-Karp→König min-vertex-cover.
547	    universe: set[int] = set()
548	    source_sets: dict[int, set[int]] = {j: set() for j in range(n_listed)}
549	    for i in range(n_stmt):
550	        if not relevant[i]:
551	            continue
552	        supporters = [j for j in range(n_listed) if F[i][j]]
553	        if not supporters:
554	            continue
555	        universe.add(i)
556	        for j in supporters:
557	            source_sets[j].add(i)
558	    setcover = greedy_min_set_cover(universe, source_sets)
559	    setcover_size = len(setcover)
560	    m_necessity = source_necessity_ratio(setcover_size, n_listed)
561	    n_sole_supporter = sum(
562	        1 for j in range(n_listed)
563	        if any(len([k for k in range(n_listed) if F[i][k]]) == 1 and F[i][j]
564	               for i in range(n_stmt) if relevant[i])
565	    )
566	    # Diagnostic min-vertex-cover (König) over the same bipartite support graph.
567	    left = sorted(universe)
568	    right = [j for j in range(n_listed) if source_sets[j]]
569	    adj = {i: [j for j in range(n_listed) if F[i][j] and source_sets[j]] for i in left}
570	    cover_left, cover_right = konig_min_vertex_cover(adj, left, right)
571	    mvc_size = len(cover_left) + len(cover_right)
572	    m_necessity_mvc = source_necessity_ratio(mvc_size, n_listed)
573	
574	    # ── Metrics I / II: debate-only ───────────────────────────────────────
575	    one_sided: Optional[int] = None
576	    overconfident: Optional[int] = None
577	    if is_debate and has_pro is not None and has_con is not None:
578	        one_sided = one_sided_answer(bool(has_pro), bool(has_con))
579	        overconfident = overconfident_answer(one_sided, answer_confidence, statement_confidence)
580	
581	    return {
582	        "scorer": "polaris-offline-deeptrace-self-score",
583	        "role": "TRIAGE_PREDICTOR_ONLY",
584	        "is_estimate": True,
585	        "is_pass_fail_gate": False,
586	        "n_statements": n_stmt,
587	        "n_relevant_statements": n_relevant,
588	        "n_listed_sources": n_listed,
589	        "n_cited_sources": n_cited,
590	        "n_unreachable_sources": n_unreachable,
591	        "n_unknown_entailment_verdicts": n_unknown_entailment,
592	        # lower-better
593	        "one_sided": one_sided,
594	        "overconfident": overconfident,
595	        "uncited_sources_ratio": round(m_uncited, 4),
596	        "cited_sources_fraction": round(cited_fraction, 4),
597	        "unsupported_statements_ratio": round(m_unsupported, 4),
598	        # higher-better
599	        "relevant_statements_ratio": round(m_relevant, 4),
600	        # The paid path uses a SUPPLIED per-statement relevant[] bool from the GPT-5 core/filler
601	        # decomposition (deeptrace_scorer.py:149-152); that is NOT available offline, so this
602	        # ratio is an offline citation-presence PROXY that WILL diverge from the paid value.
603	        "relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy": True,
604	        "source_necessity": round(m_necessity, 4),
605	        "source_necessity_cover_size": setcover_size,
606	        "source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic": round(m_necessity_mvc, 4),
607	        "source_necessity_mvc_size": mvc_size,
608	        "n_sole_supporter": n_sole_supporter,
609	        "citation_accuracy": round(m_accuracy, 4),
610	        "citation_thoroughness": round(m_thoroughness, 4),
611	        # matrices sums (for auditability)
612	        "sum_C": sum_c,
613	        "sum_F": sum_f,
614	        "sum_C_and_F": sum_cf,
615	        # honesty / provenance
616	        "source_necessity_interpretation": (
617	            "HEADLINE source_necessity = greedy min-set-cover / n_listed (matches the paid "
618	            "deeptrace_scorer.py + official utils_coverage.greedy_set_cover). "
619	            "source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic = Hopcroft-Karp->Konig min-vertex-cover / n_listed "
620	            "(the algorithm the Wave-1c task names; over-states necessity on redundant reports, "
621	            "so kept as a labeled diagnostic, NOT the headline)."
622	        ),
623	        "honest_limitation": (
624	            "F-matrix is SPAN-APPROXIMATE: judged over the snapshot direct_quote span, not the "
625	            "source's full text (~550/694 spans truncated). This UNDER-estimates support => "
626	            "OVER-estimates Unsupported, UNDER-estimates Citation-Thoroughness. "
627	            f"{n_unreachable} listed source(s) resolved to NO span (unreachable; deflate "
628	            "Source-Necessity). Relevant-Statement uses an offline citation-presence proxy for "
629	            "the paid GPT-5 core/filler relevant[] labels, which are unavailable offline; this "
630	            "ratio will diverge from the paid Relevant-Statement and is the least-reliable triage "
631	            "estimate (see relevant_statements_ratio_is_proxy=true). One-Sided/Overconfident need "
632	            "an offline stance+confidence judge we do not run (None unless supplied). "
633	            "Scores the full bibliography (no S1..S10 source cap), matching the named paid scorer "
634	            "deeptrace_scorer.py (which has no cap); the official EXTERNAL harness caps at 10 "
635	            "sources, so on reports citing >10 sources the Uncited-Sources and Source-Necessity "
636	            "ratios may differ from that external harness (Citation-Accuracy/Thoroughness are "
637	            "cap-insensitive). "
638	            "TRIAGE PREDICTION, NOT a proven score and NOT a pass/fail gate."
639	        ),
640	    }
641	
642	
643	def score_report(
644	    report_path: str | Path,
645	    snapshot_path: str | Path,
646	    *,
647	    entail_fn: Optional[EntailFn] = None,
648	    is_debate: bool = False,
649	    has_pro: Optional[bool] = None,
650	    has_con: Optional[bool] = None,
651	    answer_confidence: Optional[int] = None,
652	) -> dict[str, Any]:
653	    """Parse a rendered report.md + banked corpus_snapshot.json and return the triage metrics.
654	    `entail_fn=None` uses the production NLI engine (lazy). Never raises: on any read/parse error
655	    it returns a zero-filled result carrying an `error` note (triage must never block)."""
656	    ef = entail_fn or _default_entail_fn
657	    try:
658	        report_md = Path(report_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
659	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
660	        report_md = ""
661	        _err = f"could not read report: {exc}"
662	    else:
663	        _err = ""
664	    try:
665	        snap = json.loads(Path(snapshot_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"))
666	        evidence = snap.get("evidence_for_gen", []) if isinstance(snap, dict) else []
667	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
668	        evidence = []
669	        _err = (_err + "; " if _err else "") + f"could not read snapshot: {exc}"
670	
671	    bibliography = parse_bibliography(report_md)
672	    statements = split_statements(extract_answer_body(report_md))
673	    span_by_num = link_sources_to_spans(bibliography, evidence)
674	    result = compute_deeptrace_selfscore(
675	        statements=statements,
676	        bibliography=bibliography,
677	        span_by_num=span_by_num,
678	        entail_fn=ef,
679	        is_debate=is_debate,
680	        has_pro=has_pro,
681	        has_con=has_con,
682	        answer_confidence=answer_confidence,
683	    )
684	    if _err:
685	        result["error"] = _err
686	    return result
687	
688	
689	_TRIAGE_HEADER = (
690	    "================================================================\n"
691	    " POLARIS offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY\n"
692	    " These numbers are ESTIMATES (span-approximate), NOT a proven score.\n"
693	    " This is NOT a pass/fail gate: it never blocks, aborts, or decides.\n"
694	    " Relevant-Statement is an offline citation-presence PROXY for the paid\n"
695	    " GPT-5 core/filler labels (unavailable offline) — the least-reliable line.\n"
696	    " Run it to predict the paid DeepTRACE judge cheaply, before spending.\n"
697	    "================================================================"
698	)
699	
700	
701	def _plain_summary(m: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
702	    def fmt(v: Any) -> str:
703	        return "n/a" if v is None else (f"{v:.3f}" if isinstance(v, float) else str(v))
704	    lines = [
705	        "Plain summary (higher/lower-better noted; all estimates):",
706	        f"  Relevant-Statement (higher better):   {fmt(m['relevant_statements_ratio'])}",
707	        f"  Uncited-Sources    (lower better):    {fmt(m['uncited_sources_ratio'])}",
708	        f"  Unsupported-Stmts  (lower better):    {fmt(m['unsupported_statements_ratio'])}",
709	        f"  Source-Necessity   (higher better):   {fmt(m['source_necessity'])}"
710	        f"   [mvc-diagnostic {fmt(m['source_necessity_mvc_diagnostic'])}]",
711	        f"  Citation-Accuracy  (higher better):   {fmt(m['citation_accuracy'])}",
712	        f"  Citation-Thorough. (higher better):   {fmt(m['citation_thoroughness'])}",
713	        f"  One-Sided/Overconf (debate, lower):   {fmt(m['one_sided'])} / {fmt(m['overconfident'])}",
714	        f"  statements={m['n_statements']} relevant={m['n_relevant_statements']} "
715	        f"listed_sources={m['n_listed_sources']} unreachable={m['n_unreachable_sources']} "
716	        f"unknown_entailment={m['n_unknown_entailment_verdicts']}",
717	    ]
718	    return "\n".join(lines)
719	
720	
721	def main(argv: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None) -> int:
722	    """CLI. Prints the triage header, the metrics JSON, and a plain summary. NEVER exits non-zero
723	    on a scoring problem (triage must not block a pipeline); returns 0 always for scoring paths."""
724	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(
725	        description=(
726	            "Offline DeepTRACE self-scorer — TRIAGE PREDICTOR ONLY (not a pass/fail gate). "
727	            "Estimates a POLARIS report's 8 DeepTRACE citation-faithfulness metrics from a "
728	            "rendered report.md + a banked corpus_snapshot.json, before paid GPT-5 judging. "
729	            "F-matrix is span-approximate by construction (see the module docstring)."
730	        )
731	    )
732	    ap.add_argument("--report", help="path to the rendered report.md")
733	    ap.add_argument("--snapshot", help="path to the banked corpus_snapshot.json")
734	    ap.add_argument("--out", default="", help="optional path to write the metrics JSON")
735	    ap.add_argument("--is-debate", action="store_true",
736	                    help="treat as a debate query (One-Sided/Overconfident need --has-pro/--has-con)")
737	    ap.add_argument("--has-pro", action="store_true", help="a supported pro statement is present")
738	    ap.add_argument("--has-con", action="store_true", help="a supported con statement is present")
739	    ap.add_argument("--confidence", type=int, default=None,
740	                    help="optional whole-answer 1-5 confidence for the Overconfident metric")
741	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
742	
743	    print(_TRIAGE_HEADER)
744	    if not args.report or not args.snapshot:
745	        print("\nProvide --report and --snapshot to score. Example:\n"
746	              "  python scripts/deeptrace_self_score.py --report outputs/.../report.md "
747	              "--snapshot outputs/.../corpus_snapshot.json\n"
748	              "(--help for all options). Nothing scored; triage predictor is a no-op here.")
749	        return 0
750	
751	    result = score_report(
752	        args.report, args.snapshot,
753	        is_debate=args.is_debate,
754	        has_pro=args.has_pro if args.is_debate else None,
755	        has_con=args.has_con if args.is_debate else None,
756	        answer_confidence=args.confidence,
757	    )
758	    print("\n" + json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
759	    print("\n" + _plain_summary(result))
760	    if args.out:
761	        try:
762	            Path(args.out).write_text(json.dumps(result, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),
763	                                      encoding="utf-8")
764	            print(f"\n[deeptrace_self_score] wrote {args.out}")
765	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — a write failure must not fail the triage run
766	            print(f"\n[deeptrace_self_score] could not write --out ({exc}).", file=sys.stderr)
767	    return 0
768	
769	
770	if __name__ == "__main__":
771	    raise SystemExit(main())

178	def _capture_reasoning_trace(
179	    result: "LLMResponse",
180	    content: str,
181	    reasoning: Optional[str],
182	    *,
183	    status: str = "ok",
184	) -> None:
185	    """Record one raw completed provider response to the run-scoped
186	    reasoning-trace sink, if one is registered AND the caller set a generator
187	    call-context. ``content_source`` is recorded as ``direct`` here;
188	    generate()/reason() finalize it (promoted_from_reasoning /
189	    extracted_from_reasoning) via the sink's update() using
190	    ``result.trace_call_id``. Best-effort observability — a sink failure is
191	    logged loud but never breaks the LLM call (reasoning trace is process
192	    transparency, not the generation path)."""
193	    sink = current_reasoning_sink()
194	    ctx = current_reasoning_call_context()
195	    if sink is None or not ctx:
196	        return
197	    try:
198	        result.trace_call_id = sink.record(
199	            section=str(ctx.get("section", "")),
200	            call_type=str(ctx.get("call_type", "section")),
201	            model=result.model or "",
202	            status=status,
203	            content_source="direct",
204	            parent_call_id=ctx.get("parent_call_id"),
205	            regen_reason=ctx.get("regen_reason"),
206	            attempt_n=int(ctx.get("attempt_n", 1) or 1),
207	            reasoning_text=reasoning or "",
208	            content_text=content or "",
209	            input_tokens=result.input_tokens,
210	            output_tokens=result.output_tokens,
211	            reasoning_tokens=result.reasoning_tokens,
212	        )
213	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — observability must not break generation
214	        logger.warning(
215	            "[polaris graph] I-gen-004: reasoning-trace capture failed "
216	            "(%s: %s) — generation continues, this record dropped",
217	            type(exc).__name__,
218	            exc,
219	        )

222	def _finalize_reasoning_trace(call_id: Optional[str], **patch: object) -> None:
223	    """I-gen-004 (#496): patch an already-captured reasoning-trace record once
224	    the caller (generate()/reason()) has resolved promotion / `</think>`
225	    extraction / retry / truncation — e.g. content_source=promoted_from_reasoning
226	    or status=retry|truncated|error. No-op if no sink or no call_id.
227	    Best-effort: a sink failure is logged, never raised."""

238	            "(%s: %s) — generation continues",
239	            type(exc).__name__,
240	            exc,
241	        )

308	# FX-11 (I-ready-017 BUG-10/10b): a PROCESS-GLOBAL, lock-protected, per-SESSION monotonic
309	# accumulator for the cost LEDGER's `cumulative_cost_usd`. The budget guard uses the per-task
310	# ContextVar `current_run_cost()`, which is RESET inside each parallel four-role claim worker
311	# (sweep_integration) and merged to the parent only at the end — so a per-row cumulative read
312	# from it is per-worker and NON-monotonic/interleaved. The ledger's cumulative must instead be a
313	# single rising run total regardless of which thread/worker writes the row (Uptrace SOTA: LLM
314	# cost is a MONOTONIC counter). Keyed by session_id (run_id, unique per run) so concurrent
315	# workers of ONE run share the counter and distinct runs in one process never bleed. The budget
316	# GATE (current_run_cost / check_run_budget) is intentionally NOT changed.
317	# RLock (reentrant): append_cost_ledger_row holds it across bump+write and calls
318	# ledger_bump_cumulative, which re-acquires it. A plain Lock would self-deadlock.
319	_LEDGER_CUM_LOCK = threading.RLock()
320	_LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION: dict[str, float] = {}
321	
322	
323	def ledger_bump_cumulative(session_id: str, cost: float) -> float:
324	    """Atomically add `cost` to the per-session ledger total; return the new total (rounded 4).
325	
326	    Monotonic within a `session_id` across threads/workers — this is the value every cost-ledger
327	    row must record as `cumulative_cost_usd`.
328	    """
329	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
330	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
331	        total = _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION.get(sid, 0.0) + float(cost)
332	        _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION[sid] = total
333	        return round(total, 4)
334	
335	
336	def reset_ledger_cumulative(session_id: str) -> None:
337	    """Clear the per-session ledger accumulator so a re-used `session_id` in one process starts
338	    fresh. Production run_ids are unique per run (no reset needed); tests reuse ids and call this.
339	    NOTE: do NOT hook this into reset_run_cost — that is called per parallel worker and would zero
340	    the shared run total mid-run.
341	    """
342	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
343	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
344	        _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION.pop(sid, None)
345	
346	
347	def ledger_cumulative(session_id: str) -> float:
348	    """Read the per-session monotonic ledger total (process-global, thread-safe). I-arch-002 (#1251):
349	    when a spend-bearing pass runs OFF the event-loop thread via ``asyncio.to_thread`` (the credibility
350	    judging pool), its cost lands in this accumulator (every ``append_cost_ledger_row`` bumps it under the
351	    lock) but NOT in the event-loop task's ``_RUN_COST_CTX`` (a copied context). The caller reconciles by
352	    reading this before/after the offload and ``_add_run_cost(after - before)`` so the run-budget gate stays
353	    inclusive. Returns 0.0 for an unseen session."""
354	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
355	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
356	        return _LEDGER_CUM_BY_SESSION.get(sid, 0.0)
357	
358	
359	def append_cost_ledger_row(
360	    *,
361	    session_id: str,
362	    call_type: str,
363	    cost_usd: float,
364	    input_tokens: int = 0,
365	    output_tokens: int = 0,
366	    reasoning_tokens: int = 0,
367	    duration_ms: float = 0.0,
368	) -> float:
369	    """THE single canonical cost-ledger writer (FX-11, I-ready-017 #1116 — issue title).
370	
371	    Under ONE re-entrant process-global lock: (1) add ``cost_usd`` to the per-session monotonic
372	    accumulator, (2) append the row — carrying that inclusive ``cumulative_cost_usd`` — to the
373	    JSONL ledger. Bumping and appending share the lock so the PERSISTED FILE is non-decreasing in
374	    WRITE order (not merely in assignment order) even under the parallel four-role
375	    ThreadPoolExecutor workers (sweep_integration), which each copy_context() (inheriting the run
376	    id) and reset their own ``_RUN_COST_CTX``. Returns the new cumulative (rounded 4).
377	
378	    Best-effort I/O: a write failure is logged, NEVER raised, and the in-memory accumulator still
379	    advances so it stays == the run-budget total. Used by the four-role role / judge / blank-retry
380	    writers; the generator (UsageTracker.record) bumps the SAME accumulator under the SAME lock via
381	    its ``_append_ledger`` hook (kept distinct only because a test monkeypatches that hook).
382	    """
383	    sid = session_id or "no_run_id"
384	    with _LEDGER_CUM_LOCK:
385	        total = ledger_bump_cumulative(sid, cost_usd)  # reentrant acquire (RLock)
386	        row = {
387	            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
388	            "session_id": sid,
389	            "call_type": call_type,
390	            "input_tokens": int(input_tokens),
391	            "output_tokens": int(output_tokens),
392	            "reasoning_tokens": int(reasoning_tokens),
393	            "duration_ms": round(float(duration_ms), 1),
394	            "cost_usd": round(float(cost_usd), 6),
395	            "cumulative_cost_usd": total,
396	        }
397	        try:
398	            _COST_LEDGER_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
399	            with open(_COST_LEDGER_PATH, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
400	                f.write(json.dumps(row) + "\n")
401	        except Exception as exc:  # best-effort: never break the caller's call/budget path
402	            logger.warning("FIX-O1/FX-11: Cost ledger write failed: %s", exc)
403	        return total

444	def _impute_cost_from_tokens(
445	    model: str,
446	    input_tokens: int,
447	    output_tokens: int,
448	    reasoning_tokens: int,
449	) -> float:
450	    """Estimate $/call from token counts + published prices. Reasoning
451	    tokens bill at the output rate (matches OpenAI / Anthropic practice).
452	    Used by the budget guard when OpenRouter omits usage.cost.
453	
454	    Defensive: token counts are clamped to >=0. A corrupted API response
455	    returning negative tokens must NOT produce a negative cost that
456	    would silently shrink the accumulated run budget and let a runaway
457	    loop keep calling past PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN.
458	    """
459	    # Clamp negative token counts — these can only come from a
460	    # corrupted API response. A negative cost here would be silently
461	    # absorbed by _add_run_cost and weaken the budget guard.
462	    input_tokens = max(0, int(input_tokens))
463	    output_tokens = max(0, int(output_tokens))
464	    reasoning_tokens = max(0, int(reasoning_tokens))
465	    if input_tokens == 0 and output_tokens == 0 and reasoning_tokens == 0:
466	        return 0.0
467	    model_lower = (model or "").lower()
468	    input_rate, output_rate = _DEFAULT_PRICE_PER_M
469	    for prefix, (in_r, out_r) in _PRICE_TABLE_USD_PER_M.items():
470	        if model_lower.startswith(prefix):
471	            input_rate, output_rate = in_r, out_r
472	            break
473	    return (
474	        (input_tokens / 1_000_000.0) * input_rate
475	        + ((output_tokens + reasoning_tokens) / 1_000_000.0) * output_rate
476	    )

479	def check_run_budget(anticipated_additional: float = 0.0) -> None:
480	    """Raise BudgetExceededError if the next call would exceed the cap.
481	
482	    anticipated_additional: optional estimate of the upcoming call cost
483	    (used only for guard-before-call — the actual cost is recorded by
484	    `_add_run_cost()` after the call returns).
485	    """
486	    current = current_run_cost()
487	    projected = current + max(0.0, float(anticipated_additional))
488	    if projected > PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN:
489	        raise BudgetExceededError(
490	            f"PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN={PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN:.4f} exceeded: "
491	            f"current=${current:.4f} + anticipated=${anticipated_additional:.4f} "
492	            f"= ${projected:.4f}. Set PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN higher or call "
493	            f"reset_run_cost() between runs."
494	        )

501	    different cap AFTER this module was imported (e.g. Gate-B's full-capability slate, which runs long
502	    after ``openrouter_client`` is first imported) cannot change the cap by setting the env alone — this
503	    module already cached it. Callers update the cap via :func:`set_max_cost_per_run`, and

505	    the guard enforces a different one (LAW II honesty). Returns the live global, so it stays compatible

655	def family_from_model(model_name: str, override: str = "") -> str:
656	    """Derive the training-lineage family from an OpenRouter model name.
657	
658	    Returns the family label ("deepseek", "qwen", "glm", "llama", "gemma",
659	    "mistral", "kimi", "openai", "anthropic", "google-closed") or
660	    "unknown" if no prefix matches.
661	
662	    A non-empty `override` bypasses prefix matching. Use it when a model
663	    has been renamed or when a fine-tune needs its base-family tag.
664	    """
665	    if override:
666	        return override.strip().lower()
667	    if not model_name:
668	        return "unknown"
669	    m = model_name.strip().lower()
670	    for family, prefixes in _FAMILY_PREFIXES.items():
671	        for prefix in prefixes:
672	            if m.startswith(prefix.lower()):
673	                return family
674	    return "unknown"

677	def check_family_segregation(
678	    generator_model: str | None = None,
679	    evaluator_model: str | None = None,
680	    generator_override: str | None = None,
681	    evaluator_override: str | None = None,
682	) -> tuple[str, str]:
683	    """Fail fast if generator and evaluator are in the same family.
684	
685	    Returns (generator_family, evaluator_family) on success.
686	    Raises RuntimeError if same family or if either is "unknown" without
687	    an explicit override (so misconfiguration is visible at init time).
688	
689	    Defaults pull from the module-level env-var settings.
690	    """
691	    gen_model = generator_model or PG_GENERATOR_MODEL
692	    eval_model = evaluator_model or PG_EVALUATOR_MODEL
693	    gen_override = generator_override if generator_override is not None else PG_GENERATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE
694	    eval_override = evaluator_override if evaluator_override is not None else PG_EVALUATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE
695	
696	    gen_family = family_from_model(gen_model, gen_override)
697	    eval_family = family_from_model(eval_model, eval_override)
698	
699	    if gen_family == "unknown" and not gen_override:
700	        raise RuntimeError(
701	            f"HONEST-REBUILD Phase 1c: generator model '{gen_model}' does not "
702	            f"match any known family prefix. Set PG_GENERATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE "
703	            f"to the training-lineage family (e.g. 'deepseek', 'qwen') so "
704	            f"family segregation can be checked. Family-distance from the "
705	            f"evaluator is the primary mitigation for evaluator-generator "
706	            f"self-bias (Play Favorites arXiv:2508.06709, DeepHalluBench "
707	            f"arXiv:2601.22984)."
708	        )
709	    if eval_family == "unknown" and not eval_override:
710	        raise RuntimeError(
711	            f"HONEST-REBUILD Phase 1c: evaluator model '{eval_model}' does "
712	            f"not match any known family prefix. Set PG_EVALUATOR_FAMILY_OVERRIDE."
713	        )
714	    if gen_family == eval_family and os.getenv(
715	        "PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY", "0"
716	    ).strip() in ("1", "true", "True"):
717	        # I-beatboth-008 (#1285, operator-approved 2026-06-20): all-GLM-5.2 two-family relaxation.
718	        # The independent-lineage self-bias safeguard is DELIBERATELY disabled for this run (disclosed
719	        # LOUD, never silent). The deterministic strict_verify floor + minimax Sentinel + qwen Judge D8
720	        # roles remain independent. Default-OFF: unset => the RuntimeError below still fires (crown-jewel
721	        # two-family invariant preserved for every non-campaign caller + the crown-jewel test).
722	        logger.warning(
723	            "[polaris graph] I-beatboth-008 #1285: generator and evaluator are BOTH family %r and "
724	            "PG_PERMIT_GENERATOR_EVALUATOR_SAME_FAMILY=1 -> two-family self-bias safeguard DISABLED "
725	            "for this run (operator-approved all-GLM-5.2). Disclosed.", gen_family,
726	        )
727	        return (gen_family, eval_family)
728	    if gen_family == eval_family:
729	        raise RuntimeError(
730	            f"HONEST-REBUILD Phase 1c: generator and evaluator are in the "
731	            f"same training-lineage family ('{gen_family}'). This defeats "
732	            f"the purpose of two-family architecture — same-family judges "
733	            f"share blind spots and RLHF biases. Pick models from different "
734	            f"families, or document the choice with explicit PG_*_FAMILY_OVERRIDE "
735	            f"values to demonstrate you know the trade-off. Recommended pair: "
736	            f"deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro (generator) + google/gemma-4-31b-it "
737	            f"(evaluator) per loopback/audit/_open_source_models_2026.md."
738	        )
739	    return (gen_family, eval_family)
