742	def validate_role_families(role_map: dict[str, str], policy: str = "all_distinct",
743	                           allowed_collisions: list[tuple[str, str]] | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:
744	    """I-meta-001 (#933): N-way family segregation for the 4-role architecture.
745	
746	    Extends the pairwise check_family_segregation to N roles per the locked
747	    architecture (Generator + Mirror + Sentinel + Judge). All four roles must
748	    be from distinct training lineages by default; allowed_collisions overrides
749	    specific pairs if the architecture lock's family_policy permits them.
750	
751	    Args:
752	        role_map: {role_name: model_slug}, e.g. {"generator": "deepseek/...",
753	                  "mirror": "cohere/...", "sentinel": "ibm-granite/...",
754	                  "judge": "qwen/..."}
755	        policy: "all_distinct" (default) or "permit_collisions".
756	        allowed_collisions: list of [role_a, role_b] pairs whose families
757	                  may collide. Ignored under all_distinct.
758	
759	    Returns:
760	        {role_name: family_label} on success.
761	
762	    Raises:
763	        RuntimeError if any role's family is "unknown" or if two roles share
764	        a family outside allowed_collisions.
765	
766	    Codex APPROVE iter 2 — accept_remaining (see .codex/I-meta-001/codex_brief_iter2_verdict.txt).
767	    """
768	    allowed_collisions = allowed_collisions or []
769	    role_families: dict[str, str] = {}
770	    for role, model in role_map.items():
771	        family = family_from_model(model)
772	        if family == "unknown":
773	            raise RuntimeError(
774	                f"validate_role_families: role {role!r} model {model!r} returns "
775	                f"family='unknown'. Add the family prefix to "
776	                f"_FAMILY_PREFIXES in openrouter_client.py."
777	            )
778	        role_families[role] = family
779	
780	    if policy == "permit_collisions":
781	        return role_families
782	
783	    # all_distinct (default policy): every family must be unique across roles
784	    family_to_role: dict[str, str] = {}
785	    allowed_set = {tuple(sorted([a, b])) for a, b in allowed_collisions}
786	    for role, family in role_families.items():
787	        if family in family_to_role:
788	            other_role = family_to_role[family]
789	            if tuple(sorted([role, other_role])) in allowed_set:
790	                continue
791	            raise RuntimeError(
792	                f"validate_role_families: family {family!r} appears in both "
793	                f"role {role!r} and role {other_role!r}; family_policy="
794	                f"all_distinct forbids this. If intentional, add the pair to "
795	                f"allowed_collisions in config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml."
796	            )
797	        family_to_role[family] = role
798	    return role_families

899	def _resolve_call_timeout(
900	    model: str,
901	    explicit: Optional[float],
902	    nonreasoning_floor: float,
903	) -> float:
904	    """I-arch-004 F20 (#1255): size a per-call LLM timeout off the GENERATOR budget for
905	    reasoning-first models, instead of a small per-method default that SHADOWS it.
906	
907	    The bug this closes: ``reason()`` capped at ``LONG_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`` (180s) and
908	    ``generate_structured()`` at ``DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`` (90s) via a ``timeout or <small>``
909	    fallback. A reasoning-first model (DeepSeek V4 Pro, GLM-5.1, MiniMax-M2 — :data:`_REASONING_FIRST_MODELS`)
910	    needs MINUTES per call; the small per-method clock killed the call long before the model
911	    finished reasoning -> truncated/empty content. ``generate()`` already passes ``None`` through
912	    so ``_call_impl`` resolves :data:`GENERATOR_TIMEOUT_SECONDS`; this helper gives ``reason()`` /
913	    ``generate_structured()`` the SAME generous derivation WITHOUT regressing the non-reasoning
914	    floor (each method keeps its own ``nonreasoning_floor`` — LONG for reason, DEFAULT for
915	    structured) for the verifier / judge / side-judge calls that also flow through them.
916	
917	    Resolution order (CLAUDE.md §9.1.8 — never starve a reasoning-first call):
918	      1. An EXPLICIT caller timeout always wins (caller knows best; LAW VI override).
919	      2. Reasoning-first model + no explicit timeout -> the LIVE generator budget
920	         (``get_generator_timeout_seconds()`` so the Gate-B slate's
921	         ``set_generator_timeout_seconds()`` is honored, NOT a value frozen at import).
922	      3. Otherwise (non-reasoning-first, no explicit) -> the method's existing floor
923	         -> byte-identical to the pre-F20 behavior for those calls.
924	
925	    The $ hard cap (``PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN``), NOT the clock, is the spend backstop.
926	    """
927	    if explicit is not None:
928	        return explicit
929	    if model in _REASONING_FIRST_MODELS:
930	        return get_generator_timeout_seconds()
931	    return nonreasoning_floor

940	# glm-5.2 has 27 healthy providers — the drb_72 "Server disconnected without sending a response" bursts
941	# were OUR transport, not scarcity: the AsyncClient was built with NO limits= (httpx default
942	# keepalive_expiry=5s), so a <5s idle gap let a provider/LB close the socket while the pool still handed
943	# it back on the next call -> RemoteProtocolError. Precedent fix: entailment_judge.py:726-738
944	# (httpx.Limits max_keepalive_connections=8, keepalive_expiry tuned). These two knobs mirror that.
945	# UNSET (both) => NO limits= kwarg is passed => byte-identical to the legacy client construction.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Build the DeepTRACE Factual-Support Matrix with the signed kimi-k2.6 judge, then score.
Honest disclosure: source content fetched full-text via firecrawl; per (statement,source) the
judge sees the top-K lexically-relevant ~1500-char windows of that source (bounded context),
and returns SUPPORTED / NOT_SUPPORTED. Metrics via scripts/dr_benchmark/deeptrace_scorer.py."""
import os, re, json, sys, time, math
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
import urllib.request, urllib.error

sys.path.insert(0, '/workspace/POLARIS/scripts/dr_benchmark')
import pack_deeptrace as pd
from deeptrace_scorer import compute_deeptrace_metrics

KEY = os.environ['OPENROUTER_API_KEY'].strip()
MODEL = 'moonshotai/kimi-k2.6'
REC = json.load(open('/tmp/dt_sources/record.json'))

# ---- statements / citation matrix / relevant (deterministic, mirrors pack) ----
stmts = pd._split_statements(REC['Output'])
n = len(stmts)
n_src = sum(1 for i in range(1,11) if REC['S%d'%i])
# citation matrix C[i][j], sources 1..n_src (renumbered)
cite_sets = []
for s in stmts:
    nums=set()
    for grp in pd._CITE_RE.findall(s):
        for tok in grp.split(','):
            tok=tok.strip()
            if tok.isdigit(): nums.add(int(tok))
    cite_sets.append({x for x in nums if 1<=x<=n_src})
relevant = [bool(cs) for cs in cite_sets]
C = [[1 if (j+1) in cite_sets[i] else 0 for j in range(n_src)] for i in range(n)]

# ---- source windows ----
def windows(txt, size=1500, step=1100):
    txt=re.sub(r'\s+',' ',txt)
    return [txt[k:k+size] for k in range(0, max(1,len(txt)), step)]
SRC_WINDOWS={}
for j in range(n_src):
    t=open('/tmp/dt_sources/S%d.txt'%(j+1)).read()
    SRC_WINDOWS[j]=windows(t)

_tok=re.compile(r'[a-z0-9]+')
def toks(s): return set(_tok.findall(s.lower()))
def topk(statement, j, k=3):
    st=toks(pd._CITE_RE.sub('', statement))
    nums=set(re.findall(r'\d[\d.,]*', statement))
    scored=[]
    for w in SRC_WINDOWS[j]:
        wt=toks(w)
        overlap=len(st&wt)
        numbonus=sum(2 for num in nums if num in w)
        scored.append((overlap+numbonus,w))
    scored.sort(key=lambda x:-x[0])
    return [w for _,w in scored[:k]]

PROMPT="""You are a strict citation-faithfulness judge (DeepTRACE Factual-Support Matrix).
Decide whether the SOURCE EXCERPTS contain information that DIRECTLY SUPPORTS the STATEMENT's
factual claim. Any specific number, statistic, entity, or finding in the statement must be
present or directly entailed in the excerpts to count as SUPPORTED. Ignore citation markers like
[3]. If the excerpts do not contain the claim (even if plausible), answer NOT_SUPPORTED.

STATEMENT:
{stmt}

SOURCE EXCERPTS (from {url}):
{ex}

Think briefly, then end with a final line exactly "VERDICT: SUPPORTED" or "VERDICT: NOT_SUPPORTED"."""

def call(i,j):
    stmt=pd._CITE_RE.sub('', stmts[i]).strip()
    ex="\n---\n".join(topk(stmts[i], j))
    content=PROMPT.format(stmt=stmt, url=REC['S%d'%(j+1)], ex=ex[:6000])
    body=json.dumps({'model':MODEL,'messages':[{'role':'user','content':content}],
                     'max_tokens':1200,'temperature':0}).encode()
    for attempt in range(5):
        try:
            req=urllib.request.Request('https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions',data=body,
                headers={'Authorization':'Bearer '+KEY,'Content-Type':'application/json'})
            r=urllib.request.urlopen(req,timeout=120); d=json.loads(r.read())
            m=d['choices'][0]['message']
            txt=(m.get('content') or '')+' '+(m.get('reasoning') or '')
            cost=(d.get('usage') or {}).get('cost',0.0) or 0.0
            up=txt.upper()
            # take LAST verdict token
            verd=None
            for mm in re.finditer(r'VERDICT:\s*(SUPPORTED|NOT_SUPPORTED)', up):
                verd=mm.group(1)
            if verd is None:
                if 'NOT_SUPPORTED' in up or 'NOT SUPPORTED' in up: verd='NOT_SUPPORTED'
                elif 'SUPPORTED' in up: verd='SUPPORTED'
                else: verd='NOT_SUPPORTED'
            return i,j,(1 if verd=='SUPPORTED' else 0),cost,None
        except urllib.error.HTTPError as e:
            if e.code==429:
                if attempt==4: return i,j,None,0.0,'429'
                time.sleep(3*(attempt+1)); continue
            return i,j,None,0.0,'HTTP%d'%e.code
        except Exception as e:
            if attempt==4: return i,j,None,0.0,type(e).__name__
            time.sleep(2*(attempt+1))
    return i,j,None,0.0,'exhausted'

# cells: relevant statements x all sources
cells=[(i,j) for i in range(n) if relevant[i] for j in range(n_src)]
mode=sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv)>1 else 'full'
if mode=='pilot': cells=cells[:6]
print('n_statements=%d n_relevant=%d n_src=%d cells=%d'%(n,sum(relevant),n_src,len(cells)),flush=True)

S=[[0]*n_src for _ in range(n)]
total_cost=0.0; n429=0; done=0; errs=0
with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=8) as ex:
    futs=[ex.submit(call,i,j) for (i,j) in cells]
    for f in as_completed(futs):
        i,j,v,cost,err=f.result(); done+=1; total_cost+=cost
        if err=='429': n429+=1
        if err: errs+=1
        if v is not None: S[i][j]=v
        if done%50==0: print('  progress %d/%d cost=$%.3f err=%d 429=%d'%(done,len(cells),total_cost,errs,n429),flush=True)

print('DONE cells=%d cost=$%.4f errs=%d n429=%d'%(len(cells),total_cost,errs,n429),flush=True)
if n429>len(cells)*0.1:
    print('ABORT: sustained 429 (%d) -> matrix PARTIAL, number NOT claimable'%n429); sys.exit(3)

metrics=compute_deeptrace_metrics(citation_matrix=C, support_matrix=S, relevant=relevant,
    n_sources=n_src, is_debate=False)
metrics['_judge']=MODEL; metrics['_n429']=n429; metrics['_errs']=errs
metrics['_total_judge_cost_usd']=round(total_cost,4); metrics['_cells_judged']=len(cells)
metrics['_sum_C']=sum(sum(r) for r in C)
json.dump({'metrics':metrics,'S':S,'C':C,'relevant':relevant,'stmts':stmts},
          open('/tmp/dt_sources/result_%s.json'%mode,'w'))
print(json.dumps(metrics,indent=2))

    opaque=len(re.findall(r'cite', t)) + len(re.findall(r'citeturn', t)) + len(re.findall(r'turn\d+(?:view|search)\d+', t))
    # bracketed numeric markers [N]
    nmark=len(re.findall(r'\[\d+\]', t))
    # markdown links
    mdlinks=len(re.findall(r'\]\(https?://', t))
    # bare urls
    urls=len(set(re.findall(r'https?://[^\s)\]]+', t)))
    # numbered source-list lines "N. ... http"
    srclist=len(re.findall(r'(?m)^\s*\d+\.\s+.*https?://', t))
    # any private-use unicode chars (ChatGPT chip delimiters)
    puc=len(re.findall(r'[-]', t))
    print(f"{fn}")
    print(f"  chars={len(t)}  private-use-unicode-chip-chars={puc}")
    print(f"  opaque ChatGPT cite/turn tokens={opaque}")
    print(f"  [N] numeric inline markers={nmark}")
    print(f"  markdown links={mdlinks}  distinct bare URLs={urls}")
    print(f"  numbered source-list lines w/ URL={srclist}")
for f in ['ChatGPT_Scoped.md','ChatGPT_Unscoped.md','Gemini_Scoped.md','Gemini_Unscoped.md']:
    analyze(f)
EOF
